content: The pedagogical vocabulary learning of "reflection" as a bilingual lexical concept in high school English curricula can be repurposed as a structured alignment prompt for improving LLM multi-perspective self-reflection, where explicitly anchoring the multiple semantic senses of "reflection" (mirror-like reflection, critical reflection on errors, and reflection of internal thought) guides the LLM to activate distinct, complementary reflection perspectives instead of collapsing them into a single generic self-critique.
